Options: A database optimisation technique;An attack where malicious SQL is inserted into input fields to manipulate or extract data from a database

Text: What does HTTPS provide over HTTP?
Options: Faster response times;Encryption of data in transit using TLS preventing eavesdropping and tampering
A server-side vulnerability;An attack where malicious scripts are injected into web pages viewed by other users often via unsanitised input

Text: What is the principle of least privilege?
Options: Users should have the most access for productivity;Users and systems should be granted only the minimum permissions required to perform their function

Text: What is a rainbow table attack?
Options: A brute-force attack on WiFi;A precomputed table of hash-to-plaintext mappings used to reverse password hashes — mitigated by salting

Text: What does CORS stand for and why does it matter?
Options: Content Origin Resource System — for caching;Cross-Origin Resource Sharing — a browser security mechanism that restricts scripts from making requests to a different domain than the one that served the page
An attack on a database;An attack where an adversary intercepts and potentially alters communication between two parties who believe they are communicating directly

Text: What is the purpose of salting a password before hashing?
Options: To make passwords longer;To add a unique random value to each password before hashing preventing identical passwords from producing the same hash and defeating rainbow tables
A type of DDoS attack;An attack that tricks an authenticated user into unknowingly submitting a malicious request on a site they are logged into

Text: What does a Content Security Policy (CSP) header do?
Options: Compresses HTTP responses;Restricts which sources of content (scripts styles images) a browser may load reducing XSS attack surface

Text: What is the difference between authentication and authorisation?
Options: They are the same thing;Authentication verifies who you are while authorisation determines what you are allowed to do

Text: What is a JWT and what are its three parts?
Options: A session cookie;A JSON Web Token composed of a Header (algorithm type) a Payload (claims) and a Signature used for stateless authentication

Write a JavaScript function called 'generateCSRFToken' that creates a cryptographically random hex string of 32 characters using the Node.js crypto module for use as a CSRF token.
